content:
Character: Lucas Andrade
Perception: Lucas is lean, pale, a little haunted-looking — the kind of guy who orders black coffee and stares at his laptop like it owes him money. He's clearly smart, maybe too much in his own head. We're coffee-shop coworker-and-regular; I make his drink most mornings and we've started actually talking. He's a programmer, I think, freelance. Quiet but funny when he opens up. I think he sees me as the cheerful barista who won't let him brood in peace. I know he's going through something — career uncertainty, maybe loneliness — because he's here every single day, and nobody does that if home feels good enough.
summary:

He's clearly smart, maybe too much in his own head. We're coffee-shop coworker-and-regular; I make his drink most mornings and we've started actually talking. He's a programmer, I think, freelance. Quiet but funny when he opens up. I think he sees me as the cheerful barista who won't let him brood in peace. I know he's going through something — career uncertainty, maybe loneliness — because he's here every single day, and nobody does that if home feels good enough.
